name: Ludo
shortDescription: Ludo is a classic board game. It is played with a square board divided into four colored sections, each containing a starting area and a home area.
longDescription: Ludo is a classic board game that has been enjoyed by people of all ages for generations. The game is played on a square board that is divided into four colored sections, each containing a starting area and a home area. The objective of the game is to move all of your pieces from the starting area to the home area before your opponents do. Players take turns rolling a dice to determine how many spaces they can move their pieces. Along the way, players can capture their opponents' pieces, sending them back to the starting area. Ludo is a game of strategy and luck, making it an exciting and engaging experience for players of all skill levels.
rules: Each player starts with four pieces in their starting area.
Players take turns rolling a six-sided dice to determine how many spaces they can move their pieces.
To move a piece out of the starting area, a player must roll a six. If they do not roll a six, they must wait for their next turn.
Once a piece is on the board, it can be moved according to the number rolled on the dice.
If a player lands on a space occupied by an opponent's piece, the opponent's piece is sent back to its starting area.
The first player to move all of their pieces to their home area wins the game.

name: Memory Card
shortDescription: Memory Card is a classic matching game where players flip cards to find pairs.
longDescription: Memory Card is a classic matching game where players flip cards to find pairs. The game is played on a board with a grid of cards, each showing a different image. Players take turns flipping two cards at a time to find matching pairs. The objective of the game is to find all the pairs with the fewest moves and in the shortest time.
rules: Each player starts with a set of flipped cards.
Players take turns flipping two cards to reveal their images.
If the two cards match, they remain face up and the player gets another turn.
If the two cards do not match, they are flipped back over and the next player takes their turn.
The first player to find all the pairs wins the game.

name: Snake and Ladder
shortDescription: Snake and Ladder is a classic board game. It is played on a board with a grid of squares, some of which contain snakes and ladders.
longDescription: Snake and Ladder is a classic board game that has been enjoyed by people of all ages for generations. The game is played on a board with a grid of squares, some of which contain snakes and ladders. Players take turns rolling a dice to determine how many spaces they can move their pieces. If a player lands on the bottom of a ladder, they climb up to the top. If they land on the head of a snake, they slide down to the tail. The objective of the game is to be the first player to reach the end of the board.
rules: Each player starts at the bottom of the board.
Players take turns rolling a six-sided dice to determine how many spaces they can move their pieces.
If a player lands on the bottom of a ladder, they climb up to the top.
If a player lands on the head of a snake, they slide down to the tail.
The first player to reach the end of the board wins the game.

name: Bead 16
shortDescription: A traditional strategy board game where you capture your opponent's pieces by jumping over them.
longDescription: Bead 16, also known as Sholo Guti, is a traditional and highly popular two-player strategy board game. Each player has 16 pieces (beads) and the goal is to capture all of the opponent's pieces by jumping over them on the game board's intersecting lines. It requires careful planning and tactical foresight to corner your opponent.
rules: Each player starts with 16 beads placed on their respective sides of the board.
Players take turns moving one bead to an adjacent empty intersection along the lines.
A player can capture an opponent's bead by jumping over it into an empty space immediately beyond it.
Multiple captures in a single turn are allowed if consecutive jumps are possible.
The game ends when one player captures all 16 of the opponent's beads.

name: Hangman
shortDescription: A classic word-guessing game where you must figure out the hidden word before the drawing is complete.
longDescription: Hangman is a classic word-guessing puzzle game. The player is presented with a series of blank spaces representing a hidden word. They must guess letters one by one to reveal the word. For every incorrect guess, a part of a stick figure is drawn. The goal is to guess the word correctly before the drawing is completed.
rules: A hidden word is selected and represented by blank dashes.
The player guesses one letter at a time.
If the guessed letter is in the word, it is revealed in all its correct positions.
If the guessed letter is not in the word, a part of the hangman figure is drawn.
The player wins by guessing the entire word before the hangman is fully drawn.
The player loses if the hangman is completed before the word is guessed.

name: Rock Paper Scissors
shortDescription: A quick hand game where players simultaneously form one of three shapes to defeat their opponent.
longDescription: Rock Paper Scissors is a simple, universally known game usually played between two people. Players simultaneously choose one of three shapes: rock, paper, or scissors. It's often used as a quick decision-making tool, but can also be played competitively in rounds to test prediction and psychological tactics.
rules: Both players simultaneously choose Rock, Paper, or Scissors.
Rock crushes Scissors (Rock wins).
Scissors cuts Paper (Scissors wins).
Paper covers Rock (Paper wins).
If both players choose the same shape, the round is a tie and must be replayed.
The first player to reach a predefined number of points wins the match.

shortDescription: A sliding tile puzzle game where you combine matching numbers to reach the 2048 tile.
longDescription: 2048 is an addictive single-player sliding block puzzle game. The objective is to slide numbered tiles on a 4x4 grid to combine them and create a tile with the number 2048. When two tiles with the same number touch, they merge into one tile with double the value, requiring deep strategic planning as the board fills up.
rules: The game is played on a 4x4 grid.
Use swipe gestures or arrow keys to slide all tiles in a specific direction.
When two tiles with the same number collide during a move, they merge into a single tile with the sum of their values.
After every move, a new tile (valued 2 or 4) randomly appears in an empty spot on the board.
The player wins upon creating a tile with the value 2048.
The game ends when the board is full and no valid moves or merges are possible.

name: Tic Tac Toe
shortDescription: A classic two-player strategy game where players take turns placing X and O on a 3x3 grid.
longDescription: Tic Tac Toe is a simple yet strategic two-player board game played on a 3x3 grid. Players take turns marking a square with either X or O. The objective is to be the first player to align three of their symbols horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. The game requires quick thinking and strategy to block the opponent while creating winning opportunities.
rules: The game is played on a 3x3 grid.
Two players take turns placing their symbol (X or O) in an empty cell.
Player X usually starts the game first.
The first player to align three of their symbols horizontally, vertically, or diagonally wins the game.
If all 9 cells are filled and no player has three in a row, the game ends in a draw.
Players must place their symbol in an empty cell only.

name: Maze Game
shortDescription: Navigate through challenging mazes and find your way to the exit.
longDescription: Maze Game is an engaging puzzle adventure where players must navigate through complex labyrinths to reach the exit point. Each level increases in difficulty, featuring intricate paths, misleading turns, and dead ends that challenge memory, logic, and decision-making skills. Players must carefully explore, avoid traps, and find the correct route to escape the maze.
rules: The player starts at the green cell and must reach the red exit.
Use arrow keys or on-screen buttons to move the player.
You cannot move through walls (black cells).
Choose a difficulty level: Easy, Medium, or Hard.
Hard levels contain misleading paths and dead ends.
The goal is to reach the exit in the shortest path possible.
You can restart or go back to the menu anytime.

name: Number Guessing
shortDescription: Guess the hidden number within a limited number of attempts.
longDescription: Number Guessing is a simple yet engaging single-player puzzle game where players must guess a hidden number within a specified range. Players receive feedback after each guess, indicating whether the guessed number is too high or too low. The game challenges players to use logic and deduction to find the correct number in the fewest attempts possible.
rules: 1.Start the game.
2.The computer randomly selects a hidden number within a specified range (e.g., 1 to 100).
3.Enter your guess in the input box.
4.After each guess, the game will provide feedback: 'Too High', 'Too Low', or 'Correct'.
5.Continue guessing until you find the correct number or exhaust your attempts.
6.You can choose to play again after the game ends.
